title: Leontodon nudicalyx
taxonomy: Leontodon nudicalyx is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant is a member of the genus Leontodon, which includes several species of dandelions. The species Leontodon nudicalyx is native to the Iberian Peninsula and is often found in dry, rocky areas. It is a perennial herb with narrow, lance-shaped leaves and produces yellow flowers in the summer months. The classification of Leontodon nudicalyx reflects its close relationships with other dandelion species within the Asteraceae family.

intro: Leontodon nudicalyx, also known as the dune hawk's beard or narrow-leaved hawk's beard, is a perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Leontodon nudicalyx include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, as well as being used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
